title: Manually close individual findings
goal: Add 'mini findings resolve <id>' and 'mini findings reopen <id>' CLI subcommands that wrap the existing resolveFinding/reopenFinding store functions, so any single finding can be closed or reopened independently of a phase link.

steps.title: Add findingsResolve/findingsReopen command
steps.detail: New functions in src/commands/findings.ts taking an id list + target status. No-project guard like findingsAdd. For each id: pre-check via findFindingById; emit a distinct line — resolved/reopened, already resolved/open (benign), or no such finding (error). Return StepOutcome ok:false if any id missing/malformed; already-in-state counts as success.

steps.title: Wire resolve/reopen into the CLI
steps.detail: In src/cli.ts change findings [action] to also capture variadic ids ([ids...]), add resolve/reopen switch cases dispatching to the new functions, process.exit(1) on !ok, and update the unknown-action error to add | list | resolve | reopen.

steps.title: Update findings command help + docs
steps.detail: Extend the command .description() string in cli.ts and docs/non-interactive/findings.md to document resolve <id...> / reopen <id...> (manual close/reopen, multiple ids, idempotent on already-closed).

steps.title: Tests for resolve/reopen
steps.detail: Cover happy flip (open->resolved, resolved->open), already-in-target no-op message, unknown id, malformed id (no -n suffix), mixed batch sets failing exit code while still processing every id, and the no-project guard.
